dsl_rule.translation_notes: Channel breakout operator approximated using Donchian channels (dc_upper, dc_lower, dc_middle). Entry requires price break beyond channel boundary plus volume confirmation. Exit on return to channel midline for 2 consecutive bars represented as single exit condition on midline crossing. Volume filter uses vol_ratio_20 as proxy for volume above 20-period MA. Signal is direction-agnostic (CONTEXT_DEPENDENT) so both long and short entry conditions included.

backtest_rule.reason: Channel breakout operator approximated using Donchian channels (dc_upper, dc_lower, dc_middle). Entry requires price break beyond channel boundary plus volume confirmation. Exit on return to channel midline for 2 consecutive bars represented as single exit condition on midline crossing. Volume filter uses vol_ratio_20 as proxy for volume above 20-period MA. Signal is direction-agnostic (CONTEXT_DEPENDENT) so both long and short entry conditions included.

source_rule_text: A trader should enter positions when a channel breakout operator (CBO) identifies a trend direction in the price series with sufficient timeliness to generate profits.
